notes.i18n_global: All tables in this cluster lack dedicated i18n columns (no *_i18n or _en/_es/_zh/_el columns) except ref_document which has title_i18n/issuer_i18n/description_i18n (jsonb) — i18n coverage is N/A for all except ref_document, noted once here and not repeated per row.

findings.title: Source-only row: 'raison_sociale' in schema_unified.sql but absent from live DB
findings.evidence: SELECT code FROM public.ref_legal_type returns 16 rows; schema_unified.sql:5431 contains ('raison_sociale', 'Raison sociale', ...) in the INSERT block; live count=16, source count=17. A fresh DB from schema_unified.sql will have raison_sociale; the live DB does not. The missing row means object_legal rows keyed to raison_sociale cannot be inserted on live (FK constraint would reject them). No object_legal.type_id references raison_sociale live (confirmed: only siren=1, siret=1).
findings.recommendation: Apply a targeted migration: INSERT INTO ref_legal_type (code, name, description, category, is_required, is_public) VALUES ('raison_sociale', 'Raison sociale', 'Dénomination ou raison sociale de la personne morale exploitante', 'business', false, false) ON CONFLICT (code) DO NOTHING; — add to manifest + runbook to close the fresh-vs-live drift. This is a deploy integrity violation per CLAUDE.md §Deploy integrity.

findings.title: 'write_crm_notes' has 0 grants (user and org) while all other 10 permissions have 1 user grant each
findings.evidence: user_permission: write_crm_notes user_grants=0, org_grants=0. All other 10 codes have user_grants=1 (test seed user). The CRM module (§61) gates crm note writes on this permission. The discrepancy suggests the test-user seed was not updated when write_crm_notes was added (migration_crm_module.sql, §61). Forward-looking per SP-2 but the test-user inconsistency is a seeding gap.
findings.recommendation: Add write_crm_notes to the test-user permission grant seed (or document explicitly that this permission requires a real multi-user SP-2 grant and is intentionally absent from the single-user seed fixture). If the single test user should exercise CRM authoring, run: INSERT INTO user_permission (user_id, permission_id) SELECT '<test_user_id>', id FROM ref_permission WHERE code='write_crm_notes' ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING;

findings.title: Only 'publisher' role is in use; 'contributor' and 'reader' have 0 object_org_link rows
findings.evidence: object_org_link: publisher=837, contributor=0, reader=0. The contributor and reader roles have no semantic consumers in the live data or any write RPC. The contributor arm of the description overlay (rpc_write_org_description) checks for org membership, not ref_org_role code.
findings.recommendation: FORWARD-LOOKING — no action required now. Document in decision log that contributor/reader roles are skeleton vocabulary for multi-org publishing workflows not yet implemented. Confirm before adding any editor UI that selects these roles that there is a corresponding RPC that enforces the role semantics.

findings.title: 4 of 5 actor roles have 0 live usages in actor_object_role
findings.evidence: actor_object_role: operator=799, guide=0, sales_manager=0, receptionist=0, content_editor=0. The four unused roles are seeded and forward-looking (§48 wired actor-role authoring in the editor). No broken FK or missing data — pure zero-usage.
findings.recommendation: FORWARD-LOOKING — no action. These roles become active as actors are tagged during data enrichment passes (B1+). Confirm the editor §17 actor-role picker surfaces all 5 roles (not just operator) when adding actor links.

findings.title: Split seed source: 9 codes in seeds_data.sql, 'sur_le_parcours_de' only in migration_taxonomy_seeds_coverage.sql (manifest 8y)
findings.evidence: seeds_data.sql:986 contains 9 codes with ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING. sur_le_parcours_de is seeded exclusively in migration_taxonomy_seeds_coverage.sql:176-180 with a WHERE NOT EXISTS guard. A fresh apply that runs seeds_data.sql without running 8y would be missing one relation type.
findings.recommendation: The ci_fresh_apply.sql manifest includes 8y after seeds_data.sql (line 98), so fresh apply is correct. However, if seeds_data.sql is ever run standalone, the 10th code is silently missing. Consider backporting sur_le_parcours_de into seeds_data.sql:986 block (or add a comment cross-reference) to prevent standalone-run confusion.

findings.title: All 10 relation types have 0 live usages in object_relation
findings.evidence: object_relation has 0 rows for all 10 codes. The §15 relations editor (§29) is wired and the save_object_relations RPC exists, but no relation data has been authored yet.
findings.recommendation: FORWARD-LOOKING — no action. Track at first data entry pass (B1) to confirm the editor produces valid FK references for the relation types actually needed (based_at_site, uses_itinerary, sur_le_parcours_de are the most likely first users).

findings.title: ref_document is empty (0 rows) and has no seed — intentional data table, not vocabulary
findings.evidence: No INSERT INTO ref_document found in seeds_data.sql, schema_unified.sql, or any migration. Live count=0. The table has i18n-capable jsonb columns (title_i18n, issuer_i18n, description_i18n). 5 FK consumer tables (object_classification, object_legal, object_iti, object_sustainability_action, actor_consent) all have 0 document_id references live. API doc notes ref_document as 'Tables publiques de référence sans ligne directe extraite'.
findings.recommendation: No action — empty-by-design for this stage. Documents will be inserted as real classification certificates, prefectoral orders, etc. are attached. Ensure the first authoring pass that writes to object_classification.document_id or object_iti.status_document_id first creates the ref_document row; there is no admin UI for this yet (deferred per §59 decision log).

findings.title: All 7 review sources have 0 live usages — object_review is empty
findings.evidence: object_review count=0. The RLS for object_review was completed in §56 (manifest 8w). Review import has not been executed. All source codes (tripadvisor, google, booking, expedia, hotels_com, airbnb, internal) are seeded correctly.
findings.recommendation: FORWARD-LOOKING — no action until review import. Confirm the import pipeline maps source identifiers to these exact codes before running.

findings.title: 0 live usages in object_zone despite 24 communes seeded and FE picker wired
findings.evidence: object_zone has 0 rows. The §41 zones authoring feature is live (migration_ref_commune.sql applied, FK confirmed: object_zone.insee_commune -> ref_commune.insee_code). FE §16 multi-select loads ref_commune via client.from('ref_commune'). Zero usage is expected — no commune zones have been authored yet.
findings.recommendation: FORWARD-LOOKING — no action. The zone-authoring path is complete. First use will happen during the B1 object enrichment pass. Confirm the FE picker correctly maps the selected insee_code to object_zone rows via save_object_places({zones}).

findings.title: i18n N/A for all cluster-9 tables (no i18n columns) except ref_document
findings.evidence: ref_org_role, ref_org_admin_role, ref_org_business_role, ref_actor_role, ref_object_relation_type, ref_permission, ref_legal_type, ref_review_source, ref_commune: all have only a single 'name' TEXT column; no *_i18n jsonb, no _en/_es/_zh/_el columns. ref_document is the only exception: has title_i18n, issuer_i18n, description_i18n (jsonb) — but 0 rows, so i18n fields are unpopulated. Global i18n target (fr+en/es/zh/el) is NOT met for any table in this cluster.
findings.recommendation: These are governance/admin vocabulary tables primarily consumed server-side (DEFINER RPCs) or in the admin UI (team settings, /team page). The FR values are correct. i18n for these tables is low-value vs. tourism object content. If the admin UI or API responses ever expose role/permission names to non-FR users, add *_i18n jsonb columns via migration. Not urgent for MVP.
